text: In agile methodology, teams work in short development cycles called sprints, usually lasting one to four weeks. Each sprint begins with planning, where the team selects tasks from a backlog—a prioritized list of features and fixes. Team members describe user stories in simple language to explain what users need, like "As a customer, I want to track my order." Daily standups are short meetings where everyone shares progress and any obstacles. After each sprint, the team reviews the work and adjusts plans. This iterative development process allows for continuous feedback and improvement, ensuring the final product better meets user needs.
